author: d-kleine
text:
### 📚 The doc issue

[PyTorch Leaky ReLU documentation](https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/generated/torch.nn.LeakyReLU.html#torch.nn.LeakyReLU)

As the author in [this](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/68867228/where-is-the-negative-slope-in-a-leakyrelu) post critizises, the naming "negative slope" for the parameter in Leaky ReLU is somewhat confusing as it is not a "negative" slope (in the narrow sense, see [here](https://image.shutterstock.com/image-vector/types-slope-negative-600w-1884989446.jpg)) but refers to the negative part (quadrant III in a [cartesian coordinate system](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/Cartesian_coordinates_2D.svg/450px-Cartesian_coordinates_2D.svg.png) of the slope of the input activated by the Leaky ReLU function.

For comparison, in TensorFlow/Keras the parameter is called alpha:

> alpha | Float >= 0. Negative slope coefficient. Default to 0.3.

[TensorFlow Leaky ReLU documentation](https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/keras/layers/LeakyReLU#args)

### Suggest a potential alternative/fix

* Adaption from TF: `alpha`
* Update documentation with more precise information, that `alpha` refers to the negative part (quadrant III in a cartesian coordinate system) of the slope

cc [@svekars](https://github.com/svekars) [@holly1238](https://github.com/holly1238) [@albanD](https://github.com/albanD) [@mruberry](https://github.com/mruberry) [@jbschlosser](https://github.com/jbschlosser) [@walterddr](https://github.com/walterddr) [@kshitij12345](https://github.com/kshitij12345) [@saketh-are](https://github.com/saketh-are)

author: albanD
text:
Changing the name would be quite challenging I'm afraid due to BC reasons.  
But we can update the doc to make it clearer for sure,
